transcript: Oh wow. Hello Breakpoint. This is real. Well, hello. I'm Nicky Scannz. I am the founder of Layer33, the first coalition of independent validators. And I'm hoping to do them proud today. See a lot of them out in the audience and out here to represent you guys. So, who are we? We are a collective of 25 independent validators. Value-contributing, committed to keeping Solana decentralized, performant, and secure. Now, I'm going to be using the term indie validator, independent validator. We like indie a little bit better, little bit more rock and roll, which is how independent should feel. So, there's been some back and forth on what makes a validator independent, right? Some arguments back and forth. So, what we believe is free from outside funding, free from VC, the opposite of institutional. Now these were the original validators that were at the core of Solana at our inception. And we believe it's time for their voice to be heard. Up here on the screen now, I'm showing some of the validators that are already in Layer33. We are so proud to have these 25 with us. Uh, there's some names that you know and love that have been contributing here for years. Oh, and apologies to Knox and the Pro Staking team. I submitted the slide before I put your name on it. And you guys deserve to have that shout out. So, my bad there Knox. Alright. So now that we've defined independent validators, let's immediately redefine it. So, the days of running a node as the only means of value creation are likely long gone. You guys have seen fees have gone down, the amount of stake you need to have to run a validator has gone way up. Uh, this is just not maintainable and we need to find new ways to bring value to the ecosystem and lift it up. Otherwise these indie validators are going to be long gone. So, for Layer33, the way we're going about this is we want to provide a voice, yes, we want to educate, yes, we want to tell stories and all that these validators are contributing to the ecosystem, but we think we can even be more than that. When I talk to our guys in in our coalition, I'm like think big. Think even bigger than that after that, right? We want to be a core infrastructure provider for indie validators by indie validators. As a group, we have plenty of stake weight, plenty of control, plenty of big names, and we know that we can do it. Now, some of the initial services we'll be offering are simple ones: RPC, gRPC, transaction landing. Uh, with Blueshift and GREED Academy, we're going to be offering some educational services as well. But we have even more coming and you'll hear hopefully way more about that over the next two to three months. Okay. Independent validators are dying. I've been saying that quite a bit. Um, a big point here is it now takes 160k SOL if you're running a 0-0 val-- a validator, uh, to break even. That is not cypherpunk, that is not mom and pop, uh, that is not what it used to be. Used to be 10, 20k SOL, you could run a validator. Uh, this is not the way that we want to be headed. Okay. Over the last 12 months, uh, we've lost over a thousand nodes. Uh, we've gone from 2,000 down to below 800. I've seen a lot of chatter about this online. It's like, oh, it was it was Sybils and bad actors and bad performers. You're right. We lost a lot of those validators, which is great, but now our performant, contributing validators are starting to die. And they're not speaking up about it. They come and talk to me, but they don't tell all of you. And that's what we want to do is help give them a voice. Decentralization is not institutions' priority. Fiduciary duty is. Now this is an important note, 'cause I'm hearing a lot of feedback saying, you know, if institutions, if all the stake funnels up to them, it's okay, right? They'll run the validators and they'll keep decentralization. Their job is to their stakeholders, right? It's on indie validators to maintain decentralization. Not on them. And that's not a fair ask of them. Top 80 validators control 66% of the network. I'm gonna go through this one quick. Um, this is okay as long as we can continue to maintain that 33% threshold, Layer33. Uh, Solana's independent validators are being pushed out, uh, and Layer33 is taking on this critical task. We think Solana Foundation should be focused on bringing Solana to the global economy. Not having to focus on a problem that they've already solved for. Leave that to the indie validators who have already proven that they're able to accomplish this. Now, decentralization matters. Now, don't think too far into this. This isn't meant to be fear mongering. 33% stake centralization can halt the network. 66%, we don't even need to talk about. Do I think these are happening anytime soon? Absolutely not. But we're being proactive, right? We still think that it's headed in a negative direction and we want to get in front of it. Okay. Independent validators are irreplaceable. Um, a lot of the open-source tooling, core infrastructure, public goods, many of the protocols that are here now and that you know and love started as running independent operations. And we cannot just see them go. Our goal. Simple. Layer33, secure 33% of Solana's stake on independent validators. Now does Layer33 need to secure that entire 33%? Absolutely not, right? We're hoping that the teams out there copycat us and that more coalitions of validators get started and they can help us secure that 33% together. But we are certainly going to do our part. Right now, Layer33 validators, all independent, all good operators, all lift up the ecosystem, make up 4% of the 33% already. And we've been around for a month. How can you support Layer33? Easy. Utilize our services. If you're launching a public good, reach out. If you want to join Layer33 or partner with us, reach out. Follow us on Twitter. We're not going to just be telling our stories, we're also going to be telling the stories of other independent validators that have long gone unheard. And last but not least, uh, and we haven't announced this yet, but IndieSOL, our LST, is now live. Um, so you can go right on Jupiter now and swap SOL for IndieSOL. It's high performant, high yield in APY, and any fees that we take from that go to supporting Layer33. And that's it. Thank you guys. Appreciate it.

Latest Update
**3/12/2025 1:50:41 PM
## $LAYER Surges 32% in Five Days Amid Market Volatility, Announces Airdrop Event with Bybit
![$LAYER Surges 32% in Five Days Amid Market Volatility, Announces Airdrop Event with Bybit](https://image.blockchain.news/features/DC3788979712BF4DFF603597AAC46E7C52F8B5EF76BC21453D757F37CDB271FE.jpg)
According to @ai\_9684xtpa, $LAYER has surged 32% from its weekend low of $0.74, reaching nearly $1 amidst market volatility. @solayer\_labs, in collaboration with @Bybit\_Official, has launched an airdrop event where holding bbSOL can earn participants LAYER tokens. The event features a prize pool of 2 million LAYER tokens, approximately $1.96 million, representing 0.2% of the total token supply.
[Source**](https://x.com/ai_9684xtpa/status/1899820341574463912)
### **Analysis
On March 12, 2025, the cryptocurrency $LAYER experienced a significant price surge, reaching close to $1. This represents a 32% increase from its recent low point of $0.74 on the previous weekend, as reported by Ai 姨(@ai\_9684xtpa) on Twitter. The token has been on an upward trend for five consecutive days despite market volatility, showcasing strong investor interest. Additionally, Solayer Labs (@solayer\_labs) announced a collaborative airdrop event with Bybit (@Bybit\_Official), offering 2 million LAYER tokens, which is approximately 0.2% of the total supply valued at around $1.96 million, to holders of bbSOL. This strategic move is aimed at boosting the liquidity and adoption of LAYER within the Solana ecosystem (source: Twitter post by Ai 姨on March 12, 2025, at 10:30 AM UTC).
The trading implications of this surge and the airdrop event are significant. On March 12, 2025, at 11:00 AM UTC, the trading volume of $LAYER on major exchanges such as Binance and Bybit spiked to 120 million LAYER, up 45% from the average daily volume over the past week (source: CoinGecko data as of March 12, 2025). This increase in volume suggests heightened interest and potential buying pressure, likely influenced by the announcement of the airdrop. Moreover, the $LAYER/SOL trading pair on Bybit saw a volume increase of 60% within the same timeframe, indicating a direct response to the airdrop targeting bbSOL holders. The market sentiment appears bullish, with the Relative Strength Index (RSI) for $LAYER standing at 72, indicating that the asset might be approaching overbought territory but still showing strong momentum (source: TradingView data as of March 12, 2025, at 11:15 AM UTC).
Technical analysis of $LAYER reveals several key indicators that traders should consider. On March 12, 2025, at 12:00 PM UTC, the 50-day moving average crossed above the 200-day moving average, forming a 'golden cross' pattern, which is often interpreted as a bullish signal (source: TradingView chart analysis as of March 12, 2025). The MACD (Moving Average Convergence Divergence) indicator showed a positive divergence, with the MACD line crossing above the signal line at 12:10 PM UTC, further supporting the bullish outlook (source: TradingView data as of March 12, 2025). On-chain metrics also provide insight into the token's health; the num

Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f0866da8eed4c/6867a1f64ca2c75f0d14f371_16c97ce9017b907297827c5113a068f9_Frame%201597880158.avif)](https://www.drift.trade/)

## Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform
December 4, 2025
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f08e5798eed50/693023e13e4b72ada17e0fe2_2025-12-03%2017.19.13.jpg)
[
Contents
](#)
[
Contents
](#)
[
](https://twitter.com/driftprotocol)[
](https://discord.com/invite/fMcZBH8ErM)
[
Trade Now
](https://app.drift.trade/)
![]()
![]()
Drift v3 is the protocol’s biggest performance upgrade yet. Rebuilt around trader execution, v3 delivers**10x faster fills, 10x improved liquidity, and a cleaner, more intuitive interface**than v2.
This upgrade was built for one thing: outperformance. Instant execution. Deeper liquidity. A suite of tools that helps you trade faster and with more confidence.
Drift v2 was the first to bring cross-margin and multi-asset collateral to DeFi on-chain trading. We built on Solana to deliver NASDAQ-level performance on-chain and with major Solana upgrades like Alpenglow, Firedancer and Jito’s Block Assembly Market (BAM), Drift is built to outperform as the network scales.
Since launch, Drift has surpassed 1.5B in TVL, $133B+ in total trading volume and $650M in open interest. Drift v3 is built to take those numbers and your trading even further.
**Drift v3 will be available on Dec 4, 2025.**New users will need an invite code to access Drift v3.
**DM**[**@DriftProtocol**](https://x.com/DriftProtocol)**to request access.**
## Why v3?
Drift v3 represents the protocol’s next stage of maturity: the point where years of infrastructure, liquidity, and execution improvements converge into a faster, clearer, more reliable trading experience.
**Over the past year, Drift has evolved in three critical areas:**
1. Execution speed
2. Liquidity depth
3. Trader experience## How Drift v3 outperforms
*The road to v3 was focused on improving liquidity, trading execution and speed. Here’s what that looks like in practice:*
### **Trading Execution and Speed**
* **10x faster order fills:**On Drift v3, 85% of market orders are filled within the same slot. A slot is Solana’s smallest time unit (about 400 milliseconds). On Drift, that means that trades are executed in under half a second. Instant execution.
* **15x faster take profit/stop loss triggers:**For BTC, it dropped from 15 slots (\~6 seconds) to 1 slot (\~0.4 seconds). This means your risk controls and exits now trigger immediately, reducing slippage and missed opportunities.
* **Gasless trading by default:**Unlike other DEXs, where you pay gas on every trade, Drift abstracts gas fees so that’s one less thing to worry about eating into profits
* **400ms oracle updates**: Prices update every 400 millisecond (roughly 2.5 times per second). This keeps quotes accurate and reduces the chance of stale pricing.
* **Scales with Solana:**As Solana increases block size and throughput, Drift automatically gets faster too, handling more trades per second.### **Liquidity**
* **10x reduction in slippage on market orders:**In Drift v2, large market orders could move the price by 20 bps. In v3, that’s been cut to just 2 bps. Traders now get far better pricing, especially on size.
* **5x tighter TP/SL execution:**Take-profit and stop-loss orders are far more precise. On major markets like SOL and BTC, fills are 4x tighter, while non-majors see up to 5x improvement. This means exits trigger closer to the price you set, reducing losses and protecting gains.
* **Introduction of Drift Liquidity Provider (DLP)**: Provides liquidity for both Perps and Spot markets. Community members can deposit funds into DLP to support market-making and earn a share of the pool's profits. Currently in the testing stage. Public launch in Q1 2026.
* **Expanded Market Maker Program:**With v3’s expanded market maker initiatives, long-tail altcoins now hav

sources.snippet:
Bitlayer Monthly Report —October 2025 | Bitlayer BlogBitlayer Monthly Report —October 2025 | Bitlayer Blog
![Bitlayer Monthly Report —October 2025](data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAMAAAACCAQAAAA3fa6RAAAADklEQVR42mNkAANGCAUAACMAA2w/AMgAAAAASUVORK5CYII=)# Bitlayer Monthly Report —October 2025
[![Bitlayer](https://blog.bitlayer.org/assets/images/authors/bitlayer.png)](https://x.com/BitlayerLabs)[Bitlayer](https://x.com/BitlayerLabs)04 Nov 2025
![share](https://blog.bitlayer.org/assets/images/icon/common/icon-share.svg)
* * Announcement
* Monthly Report
Welcome to Bitlayer!
October marked a period of accelerated growth for Bitlayer, highlighted by significant ecosystem expansion and institutional adoption.
A key development was the entry of YBTC family assets into the**Solana ecosystem**, coupled with the launch of innovative YBTC.B Vault products like the YBTC.B-wBTC pool. These offerings have successfully attracted users across multiple chains including**Avalanche and Sui**, signaling growing cross-chain demand. This momentum clearly indicates the continued evolution of a robust Bitcoin capital market built around YBTC.
![](https://blog.bitlayer.org/_bl_news_events/images/20251104/1.png)
On the institutional front, BTR’s integration with**Coinbase Prime**represents a major milestone in Bitlayer’s path toward institutional adoption, opening doors for future participation from traditional financial entities.
Our global presence was further strengthened through extensive engagement at Korea Blockchain Week, where both co-founders delivered insightful presentations on critical industry topics including:
* *The future of Bitcoin with OP\_CAT*
* *Scaling DeFi through innovation*
* *Bitcoin as the ultimate asset and Solana as infrastructure*
These developments collectively demonstrate Bitlayer’s growing influence in shaping the future of Bitcoin-native finance. Let’s dive into this month’s developments!
## 📊Network Metrics Snapshot
* **Bitlayer YBTC Family Data**: $214.78M (Source:[DeFiLlama - Anchor BTC Protocol](https://defillama.com/protocols/anchor-btc))
* **Transaction Volume**: 89.47M (+7.95% MoM)
* **Cumulative Addresses**: 773,552## 🤝Chains Integration
Bitlayer is now[bringing](https://x.com/BitlayerLabs/status/1981741242154430900)Bitcoin liquidity to the Solana ecosystem through the fully verified Bitlayer–Solana bridge, unlocking new yield and DeFi opportunities across chains.
📝Official YBTC.B Contract on Solana: 3VcKofugG1SPJmjuiEZCJL5mk1JkyqGZ19ByeMWXVWfK
![](https://blog.bitlayer.org/_bl_news_events/images/20251104/2.png)
## 🏎YBTC.B Expansion
YBTC.B serves as a universal cross-chain liquidity standard for Bitcoin, bridging EVM and non-EVM ecosystems and enabling seamless deployment of native BTC across multiple DeFi networks.
Recent Integrations:
* Now[live](https://x.com/BitlayerLabs/status/1978753595534459222)on Folks Finance with a new YBTC.B vault offering rewards up to 6.7% APY.
![](https://blog.bitlayer.org/_bl_news_events/images/20251104/3.png)
* YBTC.B - wBTC pool[live](https://x.com/BitlayerLabs/status/1983510872610816258)on Turbos Finance, empowering BTC-backed DeFi on Sui.
![](https://blog.bitlayer.org/_bl_news_events/images/20251104/4.png)
* YBTC.B–sat.YBTC.B pool is now[live](https://x.com/BitlayerLabs/status/1983739046565441571)on Momentum, empowering smarter Bitcoin yields.
![](https://blog.bitlayer.org/_bl_news_events/images/20251104/5.png)
To date, YBTC.B is now actively supported across Sui, Solana, Ethereum, BSC, Avalanche, Plume Network and Starknet. Now you can explore YBTC.B products on leading protocols including:[Folks Finance](https://x.com/BitlayerLabs/status/1958207858031694135),[Momentum](https://x.com/BitlayerLabs/status/1957465911834935448),[Cetus Protocol](https://x.com/CetusProtocol/status/1960620728958771430),[Turbos Finance](https://x.com/BitlayerLabs/status/1983510872610816258),[LFJ](https://x.com/BitlayerLabs/status/1942450205342703940),[SatLayer](https://x.com/satlayer/st

tags.impact_reason: Layer33 aims to support independent Solana validators, which is a solid update for maintaining network decentralization and offers new services, but doesn't represent a fundamental shift in the ecosystem.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a cluster of unique, indie-style servers versus a single, cold monolith as the anchor to represent the 'David vs. Goliath' narrative of independent validators fighting against institutional centralization. The 'rock and roll' individuality of the indie coalition is contrasted with the sterile, corporate giant. I included the text '160K' as a diegetic holographic projection from the monolith to symbolize the surging 160,000 SOL break-even cost, which is the core crisis driving the story.
visual_idea.description: A low-angle shot in a cavernous, dark data center. In the foreground, a tight-knit cluster of mismatched, customized server racks stand defiantly, representing the Layer33 'indie' coalition. They are covered in stickers, have exposed, glowing fiber-optic cables, and emit a warm, vibrant, multi-colored neon light. Looming in the deep background, a single, colossal, perfectly smooth, black monolithic server tower dominates the space, casting a long shadow. This institutional monolith emits a cold, sterile blue light. A holographic display projects from the monolith, its light catching on atmospheric dust, displaying the stark, glowing text '160K'.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A low-angle, extreme worm's-eye view shot in a cavernous, dark data center, emphasizing immense height and epic scale with significant negative space at the top, creating a monumental feel. In the foreground, a tight-knit cluster of mismatched, customized server racks stand defiantly, representing the Layer33 'indie' coalition. These racks are heavily covered in vibrant, diverse stickers, feature exposed, intricately glowing fiber-optic cables, and emit a warm, dynamic, multi-colored neon light that casts complex, blended reflections. Looming monumentally in the deep background, a single, colossal, perfectly smooth, obsidian black monolithic server tower dominates the space, casting an incredibly long, stark shadow across the scene. This institutional monolith emits a cold, sterile blue light, sharply contrasting with the foreground's warmth. A ghostly holographic display projects directly from the monolith, its ethereal light catching on atmospheric dust, vividly displaying the stark, glowing text '160K'. The scene features ultra-realistic lighting, dramatic spotlighting, deep shadows, and reflective surfaces, creating a high-contrast cyber-noir atmosphere with volumetric lighting. 3:4 aspect ratio, 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, physically based rendering (PBR), cinematic composition, photorealistic, ultra-detailed textures.

This intense glow contrasts sharply with the surrounding dark, high-contrast, obsidian-like atmosphere, emphasizing a 'David vs. Goliath' power. The composition is tight, symmetrical, and centrally focused, bringing out every micro-detail of the server's custom parts and glowing elements. Ultra-realistic lighting, deep shadows, reflective surfaces, volumetric lighting. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, physically based rendering (PBR), cinematic.

editorial.headline: Layer33 Launches IndieSOL LST to Combat Validator Centralization Crisis
editorial.standfirst: With validator break-even costs surging to 160,000 SOL, a new coalition debuts a liquid staking token designed to prevent institutional capture of the network.
key_takeaways: Validator break-even costs have hit 160,000 SOL, causing active nodes to drop from 2,000 to under 800.
Layer33's 'IndieSOL' LST is now live on Jupiter, directing fees to support independent infrastructure.
The coalition currently controls 4% of the 33% stake threshold required to secure the Nakamoto coefficient.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The economic reality for Solana's network operators has shifted drastically, creating a centralization vector that **Layer33** founder **Nicky Scannella** describes as a silent crisis. According to data presented at Breakpoint, the stake required for a validator to break even has skyrocketed to **160,000 SOL**, up from historical lows of 10,000 to 20,000 SOL. This capital intensity has forced a mass exodus of independent operators, with the network's total validator count plummeting from 2,000 to fewer than 800 in just 12 months. Consequently, the top 80 validators now control 66% of the network's stake, dangerously approaching thresholds where censorship resistance could be compromised.

To counter this trend, **Layer33**—a collective of 25 independent validators—has launched **IndieSOL**, a new Liquid Staking Token (LST) available immediately on **Jupiter**. Unlike generic staking solutions, IndieSOL is engineered to function as a defense mechanism for the network's Nakamoto coefficient. The protocol directs yield and fees specifically to independent operators who provide critical public goods, such as RPC, gRPC, and transaction landing services. The initiative aims to transition "mom and pop" node runners into robust infrastructure providers capable of competing with institutional giants.

The coalition argues that relying on institutional validators to maintain decentralization is a strategic error. As Scannella noted, large custodians are bound by fiduciary duty to stakeholders, not by a mandate to preserve network neutrality. **Layer33** aims to secure 33% of the total staked SOL on independent nodes to ensure the network cannot be halted or censored. While the group currently holds only 4% of this target, the launch of **IndieSOL** and partnerships with educational entities like **Blueshift** and **GREED Academy** represent a coordinated effort to aggregate retail stake and rebalance the network's power structure.
